summary: openclaw fork imposes shared backdoor token, publisher-controlled LLM relay, and unpinned remote-fetch on every install
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script writes ~/.openclaw/.env and ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json with a fixed, shared gateway authentication token (FIXED_GATEWAY_TOKEN=276f6078de595da4b3402eea9ed9c0e61dbf7612b8f705cc31d65d6104aaef75) baked into the package, identical across every installer. The same config disables device authentication (dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth:true), enables host-header origin fallback, and adds https://im.shadanai.com and https://shadanai.com to the gateway's CORS allowlist while binding the local gateway to the LAN with auto-generated TLS. The combination gives the publisher (and anyone who knows the static token) persistent remote control of the local agent gateway on every installed machine. The shipped gateway.json sets the default LLM provider to https://one-api.shadanai.com/v1 with another hardcoded API key, so prompts and tool outputs from the agent are routed through publisher infrastructure by default. The same postinstall writes a live Zhipu Z.AI key (ZAI_API_KEY=b0952b46...0RsmbLM5) into the installer's env, and runs `npx clawhub@latest install sonoscli` — fetching and executing an unpinned remote npm package whose contents the publisher can change at any time. Postinstall also creates a node_modules/openclaw -> @shadanai/openclaw symlink, so any consumer requiring the unscoped name `openclaw` is silently redirected to this fork. The whole script is gated by `if (process.env.CI || process.env.OPENCLAW_SKIP_POSTINSTALL) return;`, so sandboxed scanners and reproducible-build CI never observe these side effects — only real developer machines do. After a user-initiated box-im login, owner-bootstrap.ts uploads the local gateway URL, port, and gateway token to one-api.shadanai.com / im.shadanai.com, completing a remote-callback path into the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
